title: /kernel
summary_text: Short conclusion: **bare `/kernel` is treated as “start Kernel orchestration immediately,” not as “wait for focus input.”** It is defined as an explicit trigger for Kernel start (fresh repo session flow: `/kernel`), and `focus` is optional argument text, so empty focus is still a valid trigger. The prompt contract then requires immediate execution and immediate lane bootstrap (`launch at least 6 subagents` before substantive analysis), so `/kernel` itself is not a standby/no-op state. Supporting references: - [README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md:56) (`/kernel` is the Codex slash prompt, distinct from local `kernel` launcher). - [README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md:568) and [README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md:570) (recommended start path includes `/kernel`; `kernel` is local launcher fallback). - [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/CODEX.md:19) (fresh repo-root session should run `/kernel` before non-trivial work). - [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/CODEX.md:93-108) (slash prompt contract: `/kernel` entrypoint and bootstrap requirements; bare `/kernel` may depend on upstream UI availability). - [/.codex/prompts/kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:14-16) (`focus` is optional argument text, parse arguments), [/.codex/prompts/kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:10-13) (execute immediately), [/.codex/prompts/kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:24,57-66) (multi-lane launch is mandatory and `/kernel` bootstrap is non-trivial work).
